The company faces pressure to demonstrate meaningful AI products amid competition from Meta's smart glasses and its partnership with Alphabet's Gemini for foundation-model AI. Investors will be watching for concrete, shippable AI features to determine if Apple can leverage its vast installed base to become a leader in the AI-first world.

feed.title: Apple bets its AI future on Monday's WWDC
feed.summary: Apple's upcoming Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC) on Monday is expected to unveil a significant AI strategy, including major upgrades to Siri and deeper AI integration across its software platforms. The company aims to leverage its hardware and software ecosystem to generate additional annual services revenue, with incoming CEO John Ternus's growth strategy under scrutiny.

feed.title: Taiwan telecom warns of travel eSIM use
feed.summary: Taiwanese telecommunications experts are warning against the use of certain low-cost travel eSIM services, citing cybersecurity and privacy risks. Chunghwa Telecom Chair Alex Chien and technology commentator Hung Sheng-yi cautioned that some eSIMs may route data through Chinese networks, lack proper "Know Your Customer" verification, and expose users to data interception or malicious DNS settings. They recommend consumers use trusted eSIM providers or opt for traditional roaming with their original phone numbers for better security and reliability.

feed.title: Taiwan telecom warns of travel eSIM use
feed.summary: Chunghwa Telecom and other experts are warning Taiwanese travelers about potential cybersecurity and privacy risks associated with using low-cost travel eSIMs, especially those that route data through Chinese networks. While convenient and affordable, these eSIMs may lack proper "Know Your Customer" verification, leading to unclear network origins and increased risk of data interception. Experts recommend using original-number roaming for greater security or carefully vetting eSIM providers if choosing to use them, advising caution about permissions requested and verifying carrier information.

feed.title: The spirit of Wall Street: How Freedom is bringing US market DNA to Kazakhstan
feed.summary: Freedom Holding Corp. is expanding its American division, Freedom Capital Markets, across the U.S. and actively operating on the NYSE, contributing to significant revenue growth and client base expansion. Freedom is also exporting its SuperApp ecosystem from Kazakhstan to other international markets, demonstrating a reversal of the traditional flow of financial solutions.

feed.title: Apple iPhone Users Win Class Certification for Biometric Suit
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has lost its attempt to prevent class certification for consumers alleging the company unlawfully collected their biometric information through a facial-recognition feature in its iPhone Photos app. A US District Court judge certified three classes of iPhone users, ruling that plaintiffs met the requirements for certification of their claims under the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act. The lawsuit centers on allegations that the app uses proprietary software for facial recognition without user consent.

feed.title: WIX Stock Drops To Lowest Levels Since January 2017 – Reduction In Revenue, Bookings Guidance Dampen Investor Sentiment

Despite the reduction in revenue expectations by $25 million and a $50 million reduction in bookings, Wix increased its FY2026 free cash flow projections to approximately $420 million, attributed to $150 million in annual cost savings from recent workforce reductions. The company recently cut 20% of its workforce, or 1,000 employees, citing restructuring around AI and the appreciation of the Israeli shekel.

feed.title: Apple’s Siri is finally getting a big AI upgrade

Apple also revealed plans to infuse AI across other applications like Messages, Safari, and Home, with a focus on improving performance and user interaction.

feed.title: Apple Intelligence brings powerful AI capabilities into everyday experiences

Additionally, it features an entirely new, more personal, and capable Siri AI, alongside intelligent tools for communication, scheduling, and accessibility, with developer testing starting now and general availability expected this fall.

feed.title: Apple builds Siri AI and photo editing into new Apple Intelligence

feed.title: Apple Just Announced ‘Siri AI.’ Is the Company Finally Going to Deliver a Good Siri?
feed.summary: Apple has announced "Siri AI" at its Worldwide Developers Conference, promising a significant upgrade to its virtual assistant. Key features include a dedicated Siri app for chat history, the ability to synthesize information from multiple sources, more emotive voices, and AI-powered integration with apps like Safari, Messages, and Emails for contextual actions and image processing.

feed.title: Apple users will have more control over Liquid Glass with iOS 27
feed.summary: Apple announced at WWDC that iOS 27 will give users more control over the Liquid Glass interface, introduced last year. Users will be able to customize the feature with a new slider scale ranging from "Ultra Clear" to "Fully Tinted" and Liquid Glass will also be integrated into all Apple apps. This update addresses concerns from some users about readability and distortion previously associated with the design.

feed.title: Moody's Publishes Generative AI Slide Deck on Agentic Workflows
feed.summary: Moody's Corporation recently published a slide deck at an event, detailing its generative AI strategy and "agentic workflow" solutions aimed at improving customer insights. feed.title: From Siri AI to child safety tools: Key takeaways from Apple's WWDC
feed.summary: Apple unveiled significant updates at its annual Worldwide Developers Conference, including a new AI-powered Siri integrated with Apple Intelligence, designed to understand personal context and perform tasks across apps. These announcements indicate Apple's focus on advancing AI capabilities while prioritizing user privacy and child safety.

feed.title: Apple renames Siri as ‘Siri AI’ with new push into artificial intelligence
feed.summary: Apple has announced a major overhaul of its virtual assistant, Siri, renaming it "Siri AI" and integrating new artificial intelligence features. Siri AI will leverage on-device data and the open web to answer questions and complete tasks, and will be available later this year on iPhones, iPads, and Macs, initially in English.

feed.title: Wearables data is entering clinical care as adoption grows
feed.summary: The adoption of wearable healthcare devices is significantly increasing, with new research showing 46% of Americans own a wearable and over half have discussed this data with a healthcare provider. While engagement with these devices is high, clinical infrastructure has yet to fully integrate this continuous data. The analysis highlights demographic trends in ownership and emphasizes the need to define the impact of this data on improving health outcomes.

feed.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Represents a Make-or-Break Moment for Founders
feed.summary: Apple's newly rebuilt Siri AI, referred to as Siri AI, introduces advanced conversational capabilities and cross-app functionality, integrating features that parallel many existing third-party applications. This development, known as "Sherlocking," poses a significant challenge for founders of AI tools and apps whose products offer similar functionalities, as Apple embeds these features directly into its operating system, potentially eliminating the need for separate installations. The comprehensive integration across Apple's ecosystem could disrupt the market for various AI-driven services, from writing assistants to password managers and photo editors.

feed.title: Hey, Siri: Apple just announced a long-awaited AI update
feed.summary: Apple has unveiled a significant AI update at its annual developers' conference, focusing on a revamped Siri and deeper AI integration into its operating systems. The company emphasized privacy and utility, subtly critiquing competitors' AI approaches. Despite a slight dip in share price post-announcement, analysts believe this move positions Apple to bring AI to a broad consumer base, though its success hinges on consumer adoption and execution, particularly given past delays and the competitive landscape.

feed.title: Apple debuts Siri AI as a more personal assistant built on Gemini
feed.summary: Apple has unveiled Siri AI, a revamped version of its digital assistant, and Apple Intelligence, a new underlying AI architecture for its operating systems. Siri AI is designed to be a fully conversational, systemwide agent that can understand context, access personal data, and activate apps, moving beyond simple voice commands. Apple emphasizes a privacy-first approach, using on-device processing and Private Cloud Compute, and has partnered with Google to integrate Google's foundation models with Apple's platform and privacy infrastructure.

feed.title: Apple wires Anthropic, Google and OpenAI AI into its app-building tools

These updates aim to accelerate app development, make apps more adaptive, and enhance user experience through advanced AI features and improved developer tools. Key advancements include enhanced Siri AI integration, support for on-device and server models, and tools for game and spatial developers.

Additionally, Apple significantly expanded its child safety tools, offering parents more comprehensive controls over their children's device usage, communication, and app access, emphasizing the company's role in managing digital experiences for younger users.

feed.title: Apple failed to make its AI tool to comply to EU regulations, EU Commission says
authors: Inti Landauro and Foo Yun Chee
feed.summary: EU regulators have rejected Apple's request for an 18-month exemption from Digital Markets Act obligations, criticizing the company for blaming EU rules for its decision to delay the rollout of its upgraded Siri AI in the European Union. The Commission stated that Apple failed to develop interoperability solutions meeting EU privacy and security standards, and its request for an exemption was not an option. This comes as the DMA aims to curb the power of Big Tech, with breaches potentially leading to fines of up to 10% of global annual turnover.

feed.title: Concentration makes way for dispersion
feed.summary: The article "Concentration makes way for dispersion" by David J. Eiswert, CFA, discusses a significant shift in market dynamics. feed.title: Apple Extends Private Cloud Compute to Google Cloud on Nvidia Blackwell GPUs
feed.summary: Apple is expanding its Private Cloud Compute (PCC) platform to Google Cloud, marking the first time this privacy-focused infrastructure will operate outside Apple's own data centers. This move uses Nvidia Blackwell GPUs with confidential computing capabilities and a three-layer hardware trust stack, including Intel CPUs with TDX and Google's Titan security chip, to handle demanding Apple Intelligence workloads while maintaining Apple's strict privacy guarantees. Apple retains full software control and transparency, publishing PCC binaries for inspection and extending its Security Bounty program to this new deployment.

The agreement, valued at $160 per share, will see Fox shareholders own 73% of the merged business, creating a scaled next-generation media and technology company focused on live sports, news, and the continued rise of streaming. Both companies expect the deal to be accretive to free cash flow by the second full year after closing and achieve $400 million in cost synergies.

feed.title: Telomir publishes preclinical data on diabetes treatment candidate By Investing.com
feed.summary: Telomir Pharmaceuticals announced the peer-reviewed publication of preclinical data for its compound Telomir-Zn, demonstrating its ability to restore insulin sensitivity in a zebrafish model of Type 2 diabetes. Despite a 36% share decline over the past year, the company, with a $43.3 million market cap, holds more cash than debt, which is crucial for clinical-stage drug developers. Separately, Telomir recently acquired TELI Pharmaceuticals, securing global rights to its lead drug candidate, Telomir-1, and submitted an Investigational New Drug application for it to the FDA for Triple-Negative Breast Cancer.

feed.title: Which Is the Better Tech ETF for Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stocks, State Street's XLK or Vanguard's VGT?
feed.summary: This article compares two prominent tech ETFs, State Street's XLK and Vanguard's VGT, for investors seeking exposure to artificial intelligence stocks. XLK offers concentrated exposure to tech giants like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, leading to higher recent returns due to the AI boom. VGT provides broader diversification, including smaller AI companies, while still holding many of the same large tech firms. The choice between them depends on an investor's preference for focused giant-cap exposure versus a wider market reach.

feed.title: Nvidia Stock Gets Investors Talking After First Reported Bond Offering In 5 Years – Jim Cramer Raises This Interesting Question About Buyback

The proceeds are intended for general corporate purposes, including refinancing existing debt. Jim Cramer speculated whether Nvidia might use the bond sale to fund stock buybacks, similar to Apple's strategy, as big tech increasingly uses bond markets to finance AI infrastructure investments.

feed.title: Florida Company Says Apple Smart Devices Copy Voice Command Tech
feed.summary: Acoustic Array Technologies LLC is suing Apple Inc., alleging that Apple's smart devices infringe on AAT's patented microphone systems designed for voice commands. The lawsuit, filed in the US District Court for the Western District of Texas, claims that Apple products like iPhones, iPads, and HomePods utilize the protected technology. AAT holds US Patent No. 7,092,539 related to these microphone arrays.

feed.title: Apple Seeks Dismissal of Former Employee’s Discrimination/Retaliation Suit
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is seeking to dismiss a lawsuit filed by a former employee who claims he was wrongfully fired from his technical specialist position in 2022 due to his diabetes and related health issues. Apple argues that the employee, Connor Young, was justly terminated for repeatedly failing to comply with attendance policies, while Young alleges he was targeted and harassed by a supervisor because of his medical condition. The company maintains it granted all medically-supported accommodations and consistently supported him during his employment.

feed.title: All Apple Intelligence Features Coming In iOS 27
feed.summary: Apple is set to release iOS 27 later this fall, introducing a comprehensive suite of enhanced Apple Intelligence features, many of which leverage a partnership with Google and custom Gemini models. Key updates include a revamped Siri with improved context understanding and app integration, powerful new AI editing tools in the Photos app, intelligent Safari features, and AI-powered enhancements for Passwords, Home, and Shortcuts. These advancements aim to make Apple's various applications more intelligent and user-friendly, with most features working locally or secured by Private Cloud Compute.

feed.title: Italy's antitrust regulator probes Apple over cloud services under Digital Market rules
feed.summary: Italy's antitrust regulator has launched an investigation into Apple regarding its compliance with the European Digital Markets Act. The probe focuses on whether Apple ensures third-party cloud service providers can inter-operate with its iOS and iPadOS operating systems and have equal access to components as its iCloud service. This marks the first such investigation by the Italian watchdog under the DMA, with findings to be shared with the EU Commission.

feed.title: New BlackBerry tools let governments manage Macs without public cloud
feed.summary: BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) has announced significant enhancements to its Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) platform, addressing the growing demand for sovereign endpoint control, particularly among governments and regulated industries. The new capabilities, expected to roll out in summer 2026, include on-premises macOS management, post-quantum cryptography upgrades aligning with NIST standards for FIPS 140-3 accreditation, expanded multi-tenant support, and secure AI-assisted operations. These updates enable organizations to manage diverse device fleets without reliance on public cloud infrastructure, mitigating data residency and exfiltration risks.

feed.title: TSMC and Amkor sign 10-year deal to expand semiconductor packaging in Arizona
feed.summary: TSMC and Amkor Technology have entered a 10-year agreement to significantly expand advanced semiconductor packaging and test services in Peoria, Arizona, with a $7 billion facility expected to begin production by 2028. This partnership aims to create a more robust domestic chip supply chain in the US by co-locating packaging operations near TSMC's fabrication plant, leveraging technologies like InFO and CoWoS crucial for AI and high-performance computing. The initiative is strongly aligned with the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to reduce reliance on overseas manufacturing and mitigate geopolitical risks for critical chip production.

feed.title: Citigroup (NYSE: C) sells autocallable notes paying contingent coupons through 2028

These notes offer a contingent coupon of approximately 16.75% per annum, paid only if the worst-performing underlying is at or above its 70% coupon barrier on valuation dates. The securities are subject to automatic early redemption if the worst-performing underlying is at or above its initial value on potential autocall dates, and investors face the risk of significant principal loss if not redeemed and the worst-performing underlying falls below its 60% final barrier.

feed.title: Apple to partner with Intel Corp on chips: Trump
feed.summary: Former US President Donald Trump stated that Apple has agreed to collaborate with Intel on designing and manufacturing chips in the United States. This partnership aims to diversify Apple's manufacturing base, reducing its reliance on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC), and boosting Intel's semiconductor business and reputation. The move aligns with the Trump administration's broader strategy to secure US supply chains for critical minerals and semiconductors.

feed.title: SanDisk Jumps 11%, Western Digital Rises 7% After Apple Flags “Unavoidable” Memory Price Hikes
feed.summary: SanDisk (SNDK) and Western Digital (WDC) stocks surged 11% and 7% respectively following Apple CEO Tim Cook's comments about "unavoidable" memory price hikes, indicating strong pricing power for suppliers. Both companies have seen significant year-to-date gains and reported strong quarterly results, driven by increasing demand for memory and storage in AI infrastructure. Despite bullish sentiment and analyst projections of supply tightness through 2028, some investors are concerned about potential blow-off top valuations given the rapid price appreciation and high P/E ratios.

feed.title: Apple to change iOS app distribution and payments in Brazil under regulator agreement (AAPL:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Apple (AAPL) announced changes to iOS app distribution and monetization in Brazil, following an agreement with the country's competition regulator, CADE. These changes aim to expand options for developers while introducing new safeguards. The company made this announcement on Thursday, effective for developers in Brazil.

feed.title: Trump says Apple will manufacture chips in US with Intel
feed.summary: President Donald Trump announced that Apple has agreed to partner with Intel Corporation to design and manufacture semiconductors in the United States, a move he says will boost domestic manufacturing. This partnership is part of a larger White House initiative to revitalize American silicon manufacturing and reduce reliance on overseas production. Intel's shares saw a significant jump of 9% in premarket trading following Trump's announcement, while Apple's shares rose by 0.6%.

feed.title: Apple Dominates Global Smartwatch Market
feed.summary: Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) significantly expanded its lead in the global smartwatch market, securing a 23% share in Q1 2026 with a 21% year-over-year shipment growth, driven by its refreshed product lineup and health upgrades. The overall smartwatch market saw a 4% year-over-year increase in global shipments, with a notable rebound in China fueled by brands like Huawei and growing consumer demand for advanced health features. Despite potential macroeconomic challenges, the market is expected to maintain positive growth due to the high margins of premium smartwatches.

feed.title: Nasdaq 100 Rallies, Oil Sinks To $75 On Iran Peace Deal: Stock Market Today
feed.summary: U.S. stocks, especially megacap technology and semiconductors, rallied on Thursday following a newly signed U.S.-Iran peace deal that sent oil prices tumbling to a three-month low, with West Texas Intermediate crude falling to $75 a barrel. The Nasdaq 100 significantly outperformed other major indices, climbing 1.5% due to chipmakers' strength, particularly driven by news of a potential Intel-Apple chip manufacturing pact. Conversely, IT services companies experienced declines after Accenture cut its revenue guidance, signaling cautious enterprise spending.

feed.title: Apple May Add $100 To Higher-End iPhone Models To Counter Memory Costs: Analyst
feed.summary: Bank of America Securities (BofA) projects Apple may increase prices for its higher-end iPhone models by $100 to offset rising memory component costs. This adjustment could lead to a marginal reduction in unit demand for hardware but is expected to maintain stable service margins, supported by Apple's strong balance sheet and supplier relationships. BofA maintained its Buy rating and a $380 price forecast for Apple.

feed.title: Intel Jumps 9% After Trump Signals Apple Chip Tie-Up
feed.summary: Intel's shares rose over 9% in premarket trading after former President Trump indicated a potential partnership between Intel and Apple to design and build chips in the U.S. This collaboration could significantly boost Intel's foundry business, which CEO Lip-Bu Tan considers crucial for the company's resurgence. An Apple deal would validate Intel's ambitions in chip manufacturing, building on Intel's existing relationship with the U.S. government.

feed.title: ITC Fights Apple Bid to Rehear Ruling Upholding 2023 Watch Ban
feed.summary: The US International Trade Commission (ITC) has rejected Apple's request to rehear a ruling that upheld a 2023 ban on imports of certain Apple Watch models. The ITC argued that Apple's challenge overstated the significance of the ruling for the agency's domestic-industry test and that Apple failed to meet the criteria for a rehearing. This comes as Masimo Corp., the intervener in the appeal, also urged the court to deny Apple's petition.

feed.title: Telecom Argentina SA (TEO) Earnings Forecast: Future EPS & Revenue Growth Estimates
feed.summary: This article provides an earnings forecast for Telecom Argentina SA (TEO), highlighting its current earnings forecast score, average price target, and analyst ratings. It includes peer comparisons, revenue expectations, and historical EPS performance, noting that most analysts recommend a "Buy" trend for the stock.

feed.title: Kulicke and Soffa, Microchip Technology, and Teradyne Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Kulicke and Soffa, Microchip Technology, and Teradyne jumped after President Trump announced a deal for Apple and Intel to design and manufacture chips in the U.S., boosting the semiconductor industry. Micron also saw a significant increase following analyst price target hikes. A temporary extension of the US-Iran ceasefire and lower oil prices further contributed to positive market sentiment, easing inflation concerns.

feed.title: bioAffinity Technologies closes $3.2M public offering
feed.summary: bioAffinity Technologies (NASDAQ:BIAF) has successfully closed a public offering, raising $3.2 million in gross proceeds through the sale of 4 million common shares or pre-funded warrants at around $0.80 each. The biotechnology company, specializing in noninvasive lung cancer detection tests, saw a 146% increase in sales for its CyPath Lung product in Q1 2026, despite its stock being down 89% over the past year. The offering comes amidst strong sales growth and a recent patent allowance in Mexico for one of its lung cancer tests.

feed.title: Kulicke and Soffa, Microchip Technology, and Teradyne Shares Are Soaring, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Kulicke and Soffa, Microchip Technology, and Teradyne jumped after President Trump announced Apple's agreement to design and manufacture chips with Intel in the U.S., boosting semiconductor industry sentiment. Micron Technology also saw gains following analyst price target hikes, and a new interim agreement between the U.S. and Iran eased geopolitical tensions and oil prices, further supporting the market. The article highlights that these events provided a significant catalyst for these companies.

feed.title: Bank of America Shows Strong Performance and Market Advantages
feed.summary: Bank of America demonstrates strong financial performance and market advantages, including a substantial retail deposit base and significant Q1 revenue and EPS growth. The stock exhibits a reasonable valuation and offers an attractive dividend yield, making it an appealing prospect for investors. Analysts largely maintain optimistic ratings and raised price targets, reflecting confidence in its continued profitability and growth.

feed.title: VeriSign (NASDAQ:VRSN) Given New $308.00 Price Target at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for VeriSign (NASDAQ:VRSN) to $308.00 from $285.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating on the stock. This new target suggests a potential upside of 16.58% from its previous close. Other analysts, including Robert W. Baird and Citigroup, have also increased their price targets and assigned "buy" or "outperform" ratings, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target of $327.67.

feed.title: Apple will face product gross margin pressure in FY27, says Morgan Stanley's Erik Woodring
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's Erik Woodring predicts that Apple will experience product gross margin pressure in fiscal year 2027. This information was shared during an appearance on CNBC's 'Closing Bell', where Woodring discussed the latest developments concerning Apple and their potential impact on the company's stock. The discussion is part of a broader analysis of market trends and company-specific financial outlooks.

feed.title: SpaceX receives Baa1 rating from Moody’s with stable outlook
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has assigned SpaceX a Baa1 long-term issuer rating with a stable outlook, acknowledging its leadership in orbital launches and Starlink operations. The rating is supported by balanced financial policies, transparent governance, and Starlink's role as the primary cash flow generator. While recognizing the company's financial strengths and strategic importance to NASA and the Department of Defense, Moody's noted risks associated with high capital intensity of AI infrastructure buildout and dependence on Starship V3 for long-term growth.

feed.title: Apple: Pulling the Pricing Lever
feed.summary: Apple plans to raise device prices to counteract rising memory chip costs, which have quadrupled due to AI demand. Morningstar has increased its fair value estimate for Apple to $290 per share from $270, anticipating that the price increases will lead to a net increase in revenue despite a marginal headwind to volumes. The company expects Apple's margins to rise after memory inflation slows, modeling a one-time step increase to prices.

feed.title: Intel Stock Jumps On Reported Apple Chip Partnership
feed.summary: Intel's shares surged by 10.8% after former President Donald Trump announced a deal with Apple to design and produce chips in the U.S. This partnership aims to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing, with Intel's foundry division actively seeking major collaborations. The news has significantly increased investor confidence, with Intel's stock having risen 464% over the past year.

feed.title: VOO vs. SCHD: Which Is the Smarter Buy When Inflation Is Running Hot?
feed.summary: This article compares two ETFs, VOO (Vanguard S&P 500 ETF) and SCHD (Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF), in the context of high inflation. It argues that SCHD is a smarter buy due to its focus on dividend growth, defensive sector allocations, and lower tech concentration, which makes it better suited to environments with high inflation and potential tech sector slowdowns. VOO's heavy reliance on mega-cap tech stocks is identified as a potential vulnerability.

feed.title: Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) CEO Tim Cook Warns Price Hikes Are "Unavoidable" As Memory Chip Costs Spiral
feed.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced that price increases across Apple's hardware lineup are "unavoidable" due to soaring memory and storage chip costs, driven by the massive demand from AI infrastructure. Cook highlighted the structural breakdown in global component supply chains and the shift in memory manufacturers' focus towards higher-margin AI server components, leaving consumer electronics with tighter supply and increased expenses. While specific products and the exact timing of price adjustments were not disclosed, estimates suggest a potential $270 increase for premium iPhone Pro models, leading to investor uncertainty despite Apple's year-to-date stock gains.
